highlights.text: Given the powerful bullish trigger here and seasonal strength, I was looking for higher, writing last Tuesday December 23rd at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
My general lean is to defer to the trend.
My lean is we defend and work higher to 6973-76, 6989, then onto 7002-05.” We spent all day in tight, low volatility chop with 6963 being nearly the exact high.
The cause of the pre-Christmas squeeze however was not Santa - it was the same identical factor that causes every squeeze in ES: My core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
Whenever there is what I call an elevator down sell (a vertical flush that cuts through every support) bulls always get nearly every point of that type of action paid back in the form of a squeeze.
That footprint is the Failed Breakdown.
This would mean bulls will get paid back for this action, but only when a Failed Breakdown occurs.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a powerful Failed Breakdown we saw last Monday (given to readers in advance) that produced an 80 point rip into last weeks high.

post_title: SPX Is Coiled Ultra Tight. Move Incoming. What Way? December 31 Plan
